metadata.name: aws-alb-nonprod
metadata.title: Application Load Balancer
metadata.description: Complete web architecture featuring a highly available ALB distributing traffic to EC2 backend instances.
metadata.author: InnovativeApps
features: Security Group isolation tiers (ALB vs App)

pillars.title: Operational Excellence
practices: Infrastructure as Code with Pulumi for automated, repeatable deployments
Centralized load balancer management and traffic routing
Health check monitoring with automatic unhealthy target removal
Access logs capture all requests for debugging and analytics
CloudWatch metrics provide real-time ALB performance visibility

pillars.title: Security
practices: Security group isolation between ALB and backend instances
HTTPS listener support with ACM certificate integration
Backend instances only accept traffic from ALB security group
Multi-tier security architecture (public ALB, private backends)
VPC endpoints enable private AWS service access without internet exposure

pillars.title: Reliability
practices: Multi-AZ deployment ensures high availability across availability zones
Automated health checks remove unhealthy targets from rotation
Multiple backend instances provide redundancy and failover
ALB automatically scales to handle traffic spikes
Cross-zone load balancing distributes traffic evenly

pillars.title: Performance Efficiency
practices: Elastic Load Balancing distributes traffic efficiently across targets
Connection multiplexing reduces backend connection overhead
HTTP/2 and WebSocket support for modern application protocols
Session stickiness routes repeat requests to same backend
Target group health checks ensure only healthy instances receive traffic

pillars.title: Cost Optimization
practices: ALB pricing based on hours used and data processed (~$20-30/month base)
Shared ALB can serve multiple applications reducing infrastructure duplication
Health checks prevent wasted requests to unhealthy instances
Target-based routing enables efficient resource utilization
VPC endpoints eliminate NAT gateway costs for AWS service access

pillars.title: Sustainability
practices: AWS-managed service optimized for energy efficiency
Elastic scaling prevents over-provisioning of infrastructure
Multi-AZ design maximizes resource utilization across zones
Health checks reduce wasteful traffic to unhealthy targets
Efficient traffic distribution minimizes redundant processing

use_custom_cidr.title: Use Custom CIDR
use_custom_cidr.description: Enable to specify a custom IPv4 CIDR block for the VPC

enable_ssh_access.title: Enable SSH Security Group
enable_ssh_access.description: Traditional SSH/RDP key-based access (alternative to SSM Session Manager)

ssh_access_ip.title: Admin Access IP (SSH/RDP)
ssh_access_ip.description: IP address or CIDR block (required for advanced mode)

enable_nat_gateway.title: Provision NAT Gateway
enable_nat_gateway.description: Provide internet access to private subnets

enable_isolated_tier.title: Enable Isolated Tier
enable_isolated_tier.description: Additional database subnet tier with no internet access for maximum security

flow_log_retention.title: Log Retention (Days)
flow_log_retention.description: How long to keep flow logs

enable_rds_endpoint.title: Enable RDS VPC Endpoint
enable_rds_endpoint.description: Private interface endpoint for secure RDS API access without traversing internet

enable_ssm_endpoints.title: Enable SSM Endpoints
enable_ssm_endpoints.help_text: Required for secure instance access without SSH/IGW.

project_name.title: Project Name
project_name.help_text: Name of your project for resource tagging and tracking

vpc_mode.title: VPC Selection
vpc_mode.description: Deploy into a new or existing VPC

vpc_name.title: VPC Name
vpc_name.description: Custom name for the new VPC

instance_tenancy.title: VPC Tenancy
instance_tenancy.description: The allowed tenancy of instances launched into the VPC

cidr_block.title: VPC CIDR Block
cidr_block.description: IPv4 CIDR block for the new VPC

preset_banner.title: Configuration Presets
preset_banner.description: Presets automatically configure security groups, ports, and installation scripts for common stacks. Select 'Custom' to manually define everything.

cost_banner.title: Cost Optimization
cost_banner.description: For non-prod environments, we recommend using 't3' or 't4g' burstable instances to keep costs low while maintaining performance for bursty workloads.

ami_id.title: Custom AMI ID
ami_id.description: Specify a custom AWS AMI ID

ssh_mode.title: Access Mode
ssh_mode.description: Choose between secure SSM access or emergency SSH/RDP
ssh_mode.help_text:
Standard Security Policy
SSH and RDP ports remain closed. Secure management via AWS Systems Manager (SSM) is enabled by default.

rdp_mode.title: Access Mode (RDP)
rdp_mode.description: Choose between secure SSM access or emergency RDP
rdp_mode.help_text:
Standard Security Policy
RDP ports remain closed. Secure management via AWS Systems Manager (SSM) is enabled by default.

rdp_access_ip.title: RDP Access IP
rdp_access_ip.description: IP address or CIDR block (required for advanced mode)

alb_name.title: ALB Name
alb_name.description: Unique name for the load balancer

internal.title: Internal Load Balancer
internal.description: Whether the load balancer is internal-only

target_port.title: Backend Port
target_port.description: Port on the EC2 instances to route traffic to
